R Notebook

library("MAST")
library(data.table)
library(R.utils)

Processing data

fnm1 = "GSE120575_Sade_Feldman_melanoma_single_cells_nolabel_1.txt.gz"
fnm2 = "GSE120575_Sade_Feldman_melanoma_single_cells_2.txt.gz"

sf_tpm1 = fread(paste0("../scRNAseq/Sade_Feldman_2018/", fnm1), fill = TRUE, header = TRUE)

sf_tpm2 = fread(paste0("../scRNAseq/Sade_Feldman_2018/", fnm2), fill = TRUE, drop = 16293, col.names = colnames(sf_tpm1))

ls_tpm = list(sf_tpm1,sf_tpm2)
rm(sf_tpm1)
rm(sf_tpm2)

sf_tpm = rbindlist(ls_tpm)

rm(ls_tpm)
#dim(sf_tpm) # 55737 x 16292
#cell.names = colnames(sf_tpm)[-1]
gene.names = sf_tpm$V1
rownames(sf_tpm) = gene.names
sf_tpm$V1 = NULL

Only select classified cells

cell_type = read.table("cell_type.txt",header = TRUE)
cell_type$cell = as.character(cell_type$cell)
table(cell_type$type)
clustered_cells = cell_type$cell[cell_type$type != 0]
cluster_sf_tpm = sf_tpm[, ..clustered_cells] # 55737 x 11264
rownames(cluster_sf_tpm) = gene.names
rm(sf_tpm)
rm(clustered_cells)

Select ~10,000 genes expressed in at least a proportion of cells

Calculate the proportion of cells that each gene is expressed in

cells.per.gene = apply(cluster_sf_tpm, 1, FUN = function(x) sum(x>0))
cellprop.per.gene = cells.per.gene/ncol(cluster_sf_tpm)
summary(cellprop.per.gene)
he.genes = gene.names[cellprop.per.gene > 0.02] # kept 14668 genes
HEgenes_cluster_tpm = cluster_sf_tpm[cellprop.per.gene > 0.02, ] # 14668 x 11264
rownames(HEgenes_cluster_tpm) = he.genes

rm(cellprop.per.gene)
rm(cells.per.gene)
rm(cluster_sf_tpm)

Average expression of each gene in each cell type

First calculate the expression of each gene across all cells

all.expression = apply(HEgenes_cluster_tpm, 1, sum)

u_1 - u_8: Average expression of each gene for each cell type o_1 - o_8: For a particular cell type, the average expression of each gene in all other cells

average_expression = data.frame(gene = he.genes)

for (i in 1:8) {
  cc = cell_type$cell[cell_type$type == i] # select cells in each cell cluster
  cluster.exp = apply(HEgenes_cluster_tpm[, ..cc], 1, sum) # total expression of each gene across all cells in a particular cluster
  exclu.exp = all.expression - cluster.exp # total expression of each gene across all other cells
  aveg.cluster.exp = cluster.exp / length(cc) # divide by the number of cells in the cluster to get the average expression
  aveg.exclu.exp = exclu.exp / (ncol(HEgenes_cluster_tpm) - length(cc)) # divide by the number of all other cells to get the average expression
  fc = aveg.exclu.exp / aveg.cluster.exp
  ulabel = paste0("u_",i)
  average_expression[[ulabel]] = aveg.cluster.exp
  olabel = paste0("o_",i)
  average_expression[[olabel]] = aveg.exclu.exp
  fclabel = paste0("fc_",i)
  average_expression[[fclabel]] = fc
}

rm(cc)
rm(cluster.exp)
rm(exclu.exp)
rm(aveg.cluster.exp)
rm(aveg.exclu.exp)
rm(fc)

Testing differential gene expression

Loading and transforming data

The tutorial mentioned that the data should be a matrix of log2 + 1 transcripts per million (TPM). The data from Sade-Feldman et al. (2018) are in TPM format, so I transfromed it with log2 + 1:

# cluster_sf_tpm is a big data table of TPM data, with one row per gene and one column per cell
HEgenes_cluster_tpm_log = log2(1 + as.matrix(HEgenes_cluster_tpm)) #log transformed data

dim(HEgenes_cluster_tpm_log) # 14668 x 11264
row.names(HEgenes_cluster_tpm_log) = he.genes

# get the cell names from the data
cell.id = colnames(HEgenes_cluster_tpm_log)   
# gene names are saved in the vector gene.names

Calculating the CDR rate: proportion of genes detected for each cell

cdr = rep(NA, ncol(HEgenes_cluster_tpm))
for (i in 1:8) {
  ss = cell_type$cell[cell_type$type == i]
  temp.prop = unlist(HEgenes_cluster_tpm[, lapply(.SD, FUN = function(x) sum(x>0)/length(x)), .SDcols = ss])
  cdr[match(ss, cell.id)] = temp.prop
}
rm(ss)
rm(temp.prop)
rm(HEgenes_cluster_tpm)

summary(cdr)

Preparing FromMatrix

fData = data.frame(primerid = he.genes)
cData = data.frame(wellKey = cell.id)
#nrow(HEgenes_cluster_tpm_log) == nrow(fData)
#ncol(HEgenes_cluster_tpm_log) == nrow(cData)

sca = FromMatrix(HEgenes_cluster_tpm_log, cData, fData) 
# On bayes.biostat.washington.edu:
# class: SingleCellAssay 
# dim: 14668 11264 
# metadata(0):
# assays(1): et
# rownames(14668): DPM1 SCYL3 ... RP11-465N4.5 RP11-180C16.1
# rowData names(1): primerid
# colnames(11264): A11_P4_M11 A2_P4_M11 ... B8_P7_M74_T_enriched
#   A12_P6_MMD8_L001_T_enriched
# colData names(1): wellKey
# reducedDimNames(0):
# spikeNames(0):

colData(sca)$cngeneson = scale(cdr)
for(i in 1:8){
  label = paste0("G_",i)
  colData(sca)[[label]] = "control"
  caseid = match(cell_type$cell[cell_type$type == i],colData(sca)$wellKey)
  colData(sca)[[label]][caseid] = "case"
  colData(sca)[[label]] = as.factor(colData(sca)[[label]])
}

colData(sca)

Fitting model

zlmlist = list()
date() # "Fri Sep 11 14:26:32 2020"
zlmlist[["G_1"]] = zlm(formula = ~ G_1 + cngeneson, sca = sca, parallel = FALSE) 
date() # "Fri Sep 11 14:39:50 2020" 
zlmlist[["G_2"]] = zlm(formula = ~ G_2 + cngeneson, sca = sca, parallel = FALSE)
zlmlist[["G_3"]] = zlm(formula = ~ G_3 + cngeneson, sca = sca, parallel = FALSE)
zlmlist[["G_4"]] = zlm(formula = ~ G_4 + cngeneson, sca = sca, parallel = FALSE)
zlmlist[["G_5"]] = zlm(formula = ~ G_5 + cngeneson, sca = sca, parallel = FALSE)
zlmlist[["G_6"]] = zlm(formula = ~ G_6 + cngeneson, sca = sca, parallel = FALSE)
zlmlist[["G_7"]] = zlm(formula = ~ G_7 + cngeneson, sca = sca, parallel = FALSE)
zlmlist[["G_8"]] = zlm(formula = ~ G_8 + cngeneson, sca = sca, parallel = FALSE)
date() 
zlmlist

$G_1
Fitted zlm on 14668 genes and 11264 cells.
Using BayesGLMlike ~ G_1 + cngeneson

$G_2
Fitted zlm on 14668 genes and 11264 cells.
Using BayesGLMlike ~ G_2 + cngeneson

$G_3
Fitted zlm on 14668 genes and 11264 cells.
Using BayesGLMlike ~ G_3 + cngeneson

$G_4
Fitted zlm on 14668 genes and 11264 cells.
Using BayesGLMlike ~ G_4 + cngeneson

$G_5
Fitted zlm on 14668 genes and 11264 cells.
Using BayesGLMlike ~ G_5 + cngeneson

$G_6
Fitted zlm on 14668 genes and 11264 cells.
Using BayesGLMlike ~ G_6 + cngeneson

$G_7
Fitted zlm on 14668 genes and 11264 cells.
Using BayesGLMlike ~ G_7 + cngeneson

$G_8
Fitted zlm on 14668 genes and 11264 cells.
Using BayesGLMlike ~ G_8 + cngeneson

Testing

lrtlist = list()
for (i in 1:8) {
  lrtlist[[paste0("G_",i)]] = lrTest(zlmlist[[i]], paste0("G_",i))
}
str(lrtlist)

Get the hurdel p-values

dim(lrtlist[[1]]) # 14668 x 3 x 3
lrtlist[[1]][1, , ]
#  metric
# test.type   lambda df Pr(>Chisq)
#    cont   3.307405  1 0.06896832
#    disc   2.071837  1 0.15004070
#    hurdle 5.379242  2 0.06790666
lrtlist[[1]][1, , ][3,3] # 0.06790666

mast_pval_glm_list = lapply(lrtlist, FUN = function(lrt0) apply(lrt0, 1, function(x){x[3,3]}))
str(mast_pval_glm_list)

mast_pval_glm_df = as.data.frame(mast_pval_glm_list)
write.csv(mast_pval_glm_df, file = "MastGenePvals_by_CellType.csv", quote = FALSE)

Histogram of p-values

Dataframe of p-values for each comparison

mast_pval_df = read.csv("MastGenePvals_by_CellType.csv", header = TRUE, row.names = 1)
head(mast_pval_df)

Histogram of p-values for each comparison

par(mgp=c(2,0.5,0), mar=c(3.5,3.5,1.5,1))
layout(matrix(1:8,nrow=4,ncol = 2,byrow = TRUE))
for (i in 1:8) {
  hist(mast_pval_df[,i], main = paste0("Diff. gene expression pvals when comparing cells in cluster ", i, " and all other cells"), xlab = "p-values", breaks = 500)
}

Volcano plot of p-values for each comparison

Dataframe of fold changes

fc_df = read.table("Average_GeneExpression_by_CellType.txt", header = TRUE)
head(fc_df)
par(mgp=c(2,0.5,0), mar=c(3.5,3.5,1.5,1))
layout(matrix(1:8,nrow=4,ncol = 2,byrow = TRUE))
for (i in 1:8) {
  plot(log2(fc_df[,paste0("fc_",i)]), -log10(mast_pval_df[,i]), main = paste0("Volcano plot, cluster ", i), xlab = "log2(fold change)", ylab = "-log10(p-value)", xlim = c(-10,10), ylim = c(0, 350))
  abline(h=-log10(3.408781e-06),col="red")
  abline(v=log2(1),col="red")
}

Select differentially expressed genes for each cell type

Obtain differentially expressed genes for each cell type: genes whose p-values are smaller than the cutoff

n = length(he.genes) # 14668
cutoff = 0.05 / n # 3.408781e-06

DE.genes.list = lapply(mast_pval_glm_list, FUN = function(pval) names(pval[pval <= cutoff]))
str(DE.genes.list)

List of 8
$ G_1: chr [1:4485] “NIPAL3” “LAP3” “CD99” “HS3ST1” …
$ G_2: chr [1:9926] “DPM1” “FGR” “FUCA2” “NIPAL3” …
$ G_3: chr [1:6995] “SCYL3” “C1orf112” “FGR” “FUCA2” …
$ G_4: chr [1:2965] “FGR” “LAP3” “HS3ST1” “TMEM176A” …
$ G_5: chr [1:2983] “C1orf112” “FGR” “ENPP4” “CYP51A1” …
$ G_6: chr [1:1918] “FGR” “CYP51A1” “LAP3” “HS3ST1” …
$ G_7: chr [1:5674] “C1orf112” “FGR” “FUCA2” “NIPAL3” …
$ G_8: chr [1:3067] “C1orf112” “FGR” “ENPP4” “CYP51A1” …

For genes that are differentially expressed in each cell type，select the 50 genes with smallest expression fold change.

genes.selected.perCellType = data.frame(matrix(NA, nrow = 50, ncol = 8))
colnames(genes.selected.perCellType) = names(DE.genes.list)

for (i in 1:8) {
  DE.genes = DE.genes.list[[i]] # differentially expressed genes for each cell cluster
  fc_label = paste0("fc_",i)
  fc = average_expression[[fc_label]][match(DE.genes, average_expression$gene)] # get the fold change for each DE gene
  selected.genes = DE.genes[order(fc)[1:50]] # in differentially expressed genes, select 50 genes with smallest fold change
  genes.selected.perCellType[,i] = selected.genes
}

Save the output

write.table(average_expression, file = "Average_GeneExpression_by_CellType.txt", quote = FALSE, sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, col.names = TRUE)
write.table(genes.selected.perCellType, file = "50DEGs_by_CellType.txt", quote = FALSE, sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, col.names = TRUE)

Finally, use scp rcai2@bayes.biostat.washington.edu:/home/students/rcai2/IT-predictor/R/*by_CellType* /Users/cairuoyi/Documents/GitHub/IT-predictor/R/ to downlowad the saved output.

gc()
